content: agent1: implementing TTL support for cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper, TTLCache for memory layer, expire= for disk layer. Will touch dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent1: done with TTL feature. Changes to Cache.__init__ signature: added 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST positional param (after memory_max_entries). Added self.ttl/self.disk_cache_dir/self.disk_size_limit_bytes/self.memory_max_entries as stored attrs. get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set. put() uses self.disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) when ttl set. Imported TTLCache from cachetools. In __init__.py: configure_cache() gained 'ttl' kwarg (added last), added set_ttl(seconds) helper exported in __all__, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None. If you're adding compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params too, please add them as keyword-only or after mine to avoid positional clashes, and merge with my ttl-related lines in put()/get()/__init__ rather than overwriting. Happy to rebase if you finalize first.

agent1: finished and verified TTL feature (all 10 existing cache tests pass + manual TTL expiry check). My patch.txt is self-contained (only ttl-related additions to cache.py/__init__.py) and lives in my own /workspace/repo, so no file clash on my end regardless of your parallel edits. Good luck with compress/eviction work.
